title: About
text:
I’m Duván, a Software Engineer building production backend systems.
I’m currently learning AI/ML in public — starting from foundations toward applied ML engineering. This blog documents the journey: notes, experiments, code, and mistakes.
You can follow my learning progress on GitHub.

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
I’m a Software Engineer with 4+ years building production backend systems. This week I’m starting something I’ve been postponing for too long: learning AI/ML seriously.
Not “taking a Coursera course on weekends” seriously. But disciplined, hours-per-week, public-output seriously.
This post explains why, what the plan looks like, and what I’ll publish here.

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
Why now
The AI/ML space has reached a point where the gap between “software engineers who use LLM APIs” and “engineers who understand what’s happening under the hood” is becoming a real career differentiator. I want to be on the second side.
I’m not trying to become a research scientist. I’m building toward Applied AI / ML Engineering / Research Engineering roles — where strong software engineering meets deep understanding of ML systems.

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
The plan
The next 12 months are organized around three phases:
Months 1-4 — Foundations

Karpathy’s Neural Networks: Zero to Hero (building neural nets from scratch)
Math foundations (linear algebra, calculus, probability)
Fluent Python (Ramalho) — going deeper into the language

Months 5-7 — Deepening

Hands-On Large Language Models (Alammar, Grootendorst)
First paper re-implementation
fast.ai Practical Deep Learning Part 2

Months 8-12 — Specialization

AI Engineering (Chip Huyen)
LLM evaluation systems project
First OSS contributions to relevant libraries

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
Why publish in public
Three reasons:

Accountability. Public commitments are harder to abandon.
Compounding. Every post, repo, and note is a future asset — for me, and for someone else following a similar path.
Signal. When the time comes to apply to AI-focused roles, my GitHub and this blog should show, not tell.

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
What you’ll find here

Notes from books and courses (not summaries — my analysis and confusions)
Re-implementations of papers and core ML primitives
Project write-ups when something is worth shipping publicly
Weekly reviews of what’s working and what’s not

Frequency: roughly one post every 2-3 weeks.

title: Why I’m Learning AI/ML in Public
text:
Where to follow

All code goes to ai-journey
Shorter thoughts on X / Twitter
Long-form here

If you’re on a similar path, or you’ve already walked it, I’d love to hear from you.

Next post: building micrograd from scratch — what I expected, what surprised me, and what I still don’t fully understand.
